steps.title: Identify the main idea
steps.content: The passage uses Marion Williams as one example of the rich history of Black journalism in the United States. It then explains that the National Association of Black Journalists preserves and honors that history.

steps.title: Avoid narrow details
steps.content: Choice A invents an international-overlooked claim. Choice B gives only a founding detail about the association. Choice D expands the scope outside the United States without support. The remaining choice combines the important history with the association's role in honoring it. The answer is C.
